name: Girona
summary: Girona is a charming medieval city in Catalonia that offers excellent safety for tourists, with low crime rates and well-maintained infrastructure. While petty crime can occur in tourist areas, violent crime is rare and the city maintains a welcoming atmosphere for visitors. The US State Department maintains a Level 2 travel advisory for Spain due to terrorism risks, but these concerns are minimal in smaller cities like Girona.
quickVerdict: Girona ranks among Spain's safer destinations with a crime index of only 37.85 and a safety index of 62.15. The city combines medieval charm with modern safety standards, making it excellent for solo travelers, families, and women traveling alone. While standard precautions against pickpocketing are advised in tourist areas, violent crime is exceptionally rare. The compact, walkable city center and reliable public transport system add to its appeal as a secure travel destination.

neighborhoods.name: Barri Vell (Old Town)
neighborhoods.description: The historic quarter is well-patrolled, well-lit, and bustling with tourists during the day. Evening strolls along the medieval walls are generally safe, though standard precautions should be taken after dark.

neighborhoods.name: Mercadal
neighborhoods.description: The commercial heart of modern Girona with shopping areas and restaurants. Heavy foot traffic and police presence make this a very secure area for visitors both day and night.

neighborhoods.name: Sant Pere
neighborhoods.description: Residential area across the Onyar River with the train station. Generally safe but quieter at night, so use normal urban precautions when walking alone after dark.

neighborhoods.name: Montilivi
neighborhoods.description: University area with student housing and local amenities. Safe during the day with good lighting, but can be quieter in the evenings. Standard safety awareness recommended.

neighborhoods.name: Germans Sabat
neighborhoods.description: Residential neighborhood slightly outside the center. Less tourist activity means fewer people around, especially at night. Use extra caution when alone after dark.

neighborhoods.name: Can Gibert del Pla
neighborhoods.description: Outer residential area with limited tourist infrastructure. While not dangerous, the distance from main areas and reduced lighting require additional awareness, particularly at night.

scams.name: Fake Bird Droppings
scams.description: Someone points out fake bird droppings or stains on your clothing while an accomplice pickpockets you during the distraction.
scams.howToAvoid: Be wary of helpful strangers pointing out stains. Politely decline assistance and check your belongings immediately. Walk away from the situation quickly.

scams.name: Petition Scam
scams.description: Individuals approach with clipboards asking you to sign a petition for charity while accomplices pickpocket you or demand donations.
scams.howToAvoid: Never stop for petition collectors in tourist areas. A firm 'No gracias' and keep walking. Real charities don't collect signatures from tourists on the street.

scams.name: Tourist Menu Overcharge
scams.description: Restaurants near tourist sites offer menus without prices or inflate bills for visitors, especially around the cathedral and Game of Thrones filming locations.
scams.howToAvoid: Always check that menus have clear prices listed. Ask for prices if unclear. Pay with a card to create a paper trail. Choose restaurants frequented by locals.

scams.name: ATM Skimming
scams.description: Card readers or cameras placed on ATMs to steal card information, though less common in smaller cities like Girona.
scams.howToAvoid: Use ATMs inside banks when possible. Cover your PIN when entering. Check for unusual devices attached to card slots. Monitor bank statements regularly.

soloFemale.overview: Girona is very safe for solo female travelers, ranking 10th safest in Spain for women traveling alone with a 4.5/5 safety rating. The city has low rates of street harassment, frequent police patrols, and well-lit tourist areas. Local attitudes are respectful, and women can comfortably explore the medieval quarters, dine alone, and use public transport without significant concerns.
tips: Stay in well-reviewed accommodations in the Barri Vell or Mercadal areas
The tourist areas and main plazas remain lively until late, providing natural safety
Use official taxi services or the Gitaxi app rather than accepting rides from strangers
Trust your instincts and avoid overly quiet areas late at night
Connect with other travelers at hostels or join walking tours to meet people

nightSafety.overview: Girona is generally safe at night with a moderate safety rating of 50% after dark. The old town and main tourist areas remain well-lit and have regular police patrols. While violent crime is rare, the quieter nature of this smaller city means fewer people are around late at night compared to Barcelona or Madrid. Standard urban precautions are recommended.
tips: Stick to well-lit main streets and avoid narrow alleyways late at night
The area around Placa de la Independencia and the cathedral stays active until around midnight
Use taxis for longer distances after 11 PM, especially when alone
Bars and restaurants close earlier than in larger Spanish cities, typically by 1-2 AM
Walk with confidence and purpose, avoiding displays of expensive electronics

transport.metro: Girona does not have a metro system. The city is compact and easily walkable, with most attractions within 15-20 minutes on foot.
transport.rideshare: Uber is not available in Girona. Use the local Gitaxi app or call official taxi services. Licensed taxis are readily available and regulated by local government.
transport.taxis: Taxis are safe, metered, and well-regulated. Find taxi ranks at the train station, bus station, and Placa de la Independencia. Always ensure the meter is running. Fares are fixed for airport transfers.
transport.tips: Public buses are safe and efficient with 10 local routes covering all neighborhoods. Walking is the best way to explore the historic center. Bicycle rentals are popular and the city has good cycling infrastructure.

customs: Greet with a handshake for formal meetings or two kisses on the cheeks for friends and family (starting from the right)
Learn basic Catalan phrases like 'Hola' and 'Gracies' as Girona is in Catalonia where Catalan culture is strong
Respect meal times - lunch around 2 PM and dinner not before 8-9 PM, with many restaurants closing during siesta hours
Dress modestly when visiting churches and religious sites, covering shoulders and knees
Tipping is not mandatory but 5-10% is appreciated for good service in restaurants

health.overview: Spain has excellent healthcare with modern facilities. Girona has well-equipped hospitals and pharmacies throughout the city. EU citizens can use the European Health Insurance Card, while others should have travel insurance.
health.water: Tap water is safe to drink throughout Girona and meets EU standards. Bottled water is widely available but not necessary.
health.vaccinations: No special vaccinations required for Spain. Routine vaccinations should be up to date. No malaria or tropical disease risks.
health.altitude: Girona is at sea level with a Mediterranean climate. Summer temperatures can reach 30°C (86°F). No altitude-related health concerns.

emergency.usEmbassy: US Embassy Madrid: +34 91 587 2200. Located at Calle de Serrano 75, Madrid. 24/7 emergency line for US citizens: +34 91 714 6300

faq.a: Yes, Girona is very safe for tourists with low crime rates and a strong police presence. While petty theft can occur in tourist areas, violent crime is rare. The city has a safety index of 62.15 and is considered one of Spain's safer destinations.

Girona is generally safe at night, especially in the well-lit tourist areas around the old town and main plazas. However, as a smaller city, it becomes quieter after midnight, so standard precautions are recommended when walking alone late at night.

faq.q: Is Girona safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Yes, Girona ranks 10th safest in Spain for solo female travelers with a 4.5/5 safety rating. Street harassment is uncommon, police patrol regularly, and the local culture is respectful. Women can comfortably explore, dine alone, and use public transport.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Girona?
faq.a: No areas in Girona are particularly dangerous, but exercise extra caution in outer residential neighborhoods like Germans Sabat and Can Gibert del Pla at night due to reduced lighting and fewer people around. Stick to central areas after dark.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Girona?
faq.a: Yes, public transport in Girona is very safe. The city has 10 local bus routes with modern, air-conditioned buses. The train station connects to Barcelona and other major cities. Taxis are regulated and reliable. Most visitors find walking the most convenient option.
